transcript.text: Tucson sometimes, and Washington the other times.
It depends upon the weather.
I'm assuming right now Tucson, because the weather in Washington sucks.
Yeah, yeah, except it's actually raining right now here.
But yesterday was nice.
I don't know.
It's hit and miss, to be honest.
Why do you flip back and forth?
Or is it just winter, wet weather?
This call may be recorded for note-taking and coaching purposes.
I love Tucson, because I like to golf.
Oh, got you.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
No, it's a great place for that, and the weather.
So, yeah, I'm from Washington, so, you know, eventually you get sick of it after spending a lifetime in the dreariness.
What part of Washington?
And that has got to be the biggest coffee mug I've ever seen in my entire life.
Yeah, it's only holding tea, unfortunately.
But my security tea.
So, from Olympia, originally.
I've in Seattle and a bunch of other towns throughout my life.
Nice.
lived in Seattle for five years when I was working with Amazon.
Oh, there you go.
In the Roosevelt area.
Oh, nice.
Yeah, yeah.
I lived in that area for a little bit myself.
Oh, really?
Oh, super cool.
It's not a great area.
Now they've got the light rail that goes right up to Roosevelt High School.
Yes.
Super easy to get around.

So, Stevie, just as a quick introduction, if you'd be willing to just introduce yourself, tell us a little about your role, and then Tom and I can do the same, and then we can kick off into doing our best to adding value today.
Yeah, no, sounds good.
So, yeah, I come from more of a machine learning MLLots background, joined GrowthX last June.
And had already held a variety of roles, because I'm used to the startup life where, you know, you wear lots of different hats.
So, yeah, I'm pretty comfortable with, you know, the MLOps side of things.
So I've been ramping up on SEO, AEO, and, you know, visibility metrics and content generation.
So it's been kind of a wild ride, but it's been super fun to learn something new.
Wow, that's awesome.
Thanks.
I like it when there's a little bit more personality in the intro.
And that had the personality I was looking for.
It's really nice to meet you.
My name is Mike.
I'm based out of San Francisco.
And I've been working at Bright Data now for three years, all in a sales capacity.
I come from a technical background at AWS, but I realized the bills weren't going to get paid if I didn't go to sales.
And then Tom, I'll have you introduce yourself.
Amazing.
Very hard.
Hard to follow you too.
But yeah, recently joined Bright Data.
Prior to this, I spent time at two startups, one unicorn.
So I learned a lot in the tech space.
Prior to that, I started my career at Goldman Sachs.
So I've done a little bit of everything, but not quite as impressive as you, Stevie.
Or Michael, that matters.
Well, Stevie, I'm really excited to have the opportunity to work with you.
If you don't mind, how can we maximize value for you?
Obviously, this is not the only call you can have with us.
After this, you'll get a link to my calendar.
If necessary, we're happy to start a Slack channel.
That's a preferred communication.
But yeah, I just want to understand where can we provide value to you today?
Yeah, so we're really looking at data vendors with AI visibility metrics, like mentioned citations, volume, keyword volume, search volume.
And, you know, we really are looking to understand what AI platforms, AI engines are covered.

What the data collection methodology looks like, whether it's panel or, you know, probing, statistical modeling, just kind of trying to get our heads around how people are getting this data and how accurate it is.
And then, of course, pricing and then licensing, you know, because we're looking to embed and redistribute that data to our end customers.
So understanding, like, what your licensing looks like.
Yeah, absolutely.
That's a fantastic start.
Do you mind if I ask, are you working with data vendors today that are in the scraping business?
Or is this kind of the first foray the organization's taking?
Yeah, we're just starting to, I think, like, about a week ago, I started looking into it on a new initiative.
So, yeah.
Cool.
Very early on.
Perfect.
Perfect.
We'll see.
Thank you for giving us an opportunity.
Just from a high level, there's only one organization in the world that scrapes more data than Bright Data.
That's Google.
We scrape roughly 2.5 petabytes per day.
And the real value prop that we bring to the table is compliance and following the rules of the Internet.
We were sued actually by Elon Musk.
We were sued by Facebook and both cases didn't even go to court because we document so well with our compliance and legal team how we operate as an organization.
So we really like when it comes down to the nuts and bolts, when it's like why I work with Bright Data, we care a lot about compliance and how we scrape.
We will never go behind a login screen and we will be very transparent with you on the terms and services we are and are not accepting.
As you probably know, every browser.
So hits an accept button of fair use terms without us even realizing it.
And we're really good at saying, no, we're not going to accept that.
And that gives our customers a lot of control on where and how they scrape so that they understand the risks they're taking on by taking on certain pieces of data.
Taking that a step further, not that this is necessarily what you're looking for, but just to give you context of how we're structured as an organization.
We started out as a proxy company and we have the largest proxy network in the world.
So I tell you that because some people that are really smart like yourself are like, I can actually build a scraper better than what Bright Data can build.
And we're like, that's understandable.
Some AI folks that we're working with, like some of the AI folks I'm working with across San Francisco right now.
I'm like, you should just use our proxy network.
You should build some really cool tools.
And
So at our base layer, we give max control over the customer and say, if you just want to use our proxy network to peer from anywhere and use your own tools, we make that available to you.
Because all of our tooling is built on that best-in-class proxy network.
A really noticeable difference within our network is every single device that's on our proxy network is actually contracted.

So where our competitors are like, they're hopping from random device to random device, hoping they don't get caught, every single device, whether it's residential, ISP, or data center in our network is completely contracted.
Going to the next layer, everything that we do is API-based.
So, and it's charged based on successful records returned.
So I'm just going to pause there.
We make it super simple for you to plug keys into your existing application stack.
And have it make calls to our APIs, and then those APIs return data based on what you're looking for.

whether that's a web scraper, whether that's a search engine result scraper, whether that's scraping Travelocity, scraping Google News, my philosophy or what I tell customers is, if it's available on the public web, we can scrape it.
As long as you're not asking us to go to behind a login screen, we can scrape it.
What you do with that data is completely up to you.
That data does not belong to us.
There's no licensing behind it.
That data belongs to you, and you can use it in perpetuity for as long as you want.
Where there's continuous scraping, that also belongs to you.
So if let's say you're scraping something on a daily basis, as soon as it's scraped and it's in your inbox, that data belongs to you, and you're not paying us any type of licensing or anything.
You're simply paying us for successful returned scrapes.
You're not – you're not having to pay for
For 404 error screens and everything else that can come in between your data set.
When it comes to the scraping tools, we have APIs built already for the most popular websites.
So think of your Claude, think of your ChatGPT, think of your Co-Pilot, think of your Walmarts, your Amazons, your Kroger's.
Because at its base, we really started out as a price scraper.
Amazon came to us and they're like, can you scrape everything that Walmart has?
And we were like, yes.
Now where we're shifting is customers coming to us and saying, we need every video YouTube has to offer to train our AI models.
Can you get that for us?
So when you think about APIs, think of the most popular websites.
And then we have all these side tools like browser APIs and unlockers.
To watch.
Work with sites that aren't necessarily within our API toolkit, but we make it really easy for you to go, oh, this is the HTML of that site.
It has a lot of captures or blockers.
Bright Data's got the tools to go get through those captures at scale and then retrieve the data at scale, whether there's an API for it or not.
Yeah, and I'm assuming you also capture AI overview and then citations and mentions.
Or, I mean, I'm assuming this particular product with the scraper just does a dump of the raw HTML, but it contains that.
Yeah, citations we'll need to dive into a little bit more.
Are you looking for like query fanouts and things like that?
Yeah.
Yep.
So we're actually the third person this week that I'm talking to about query fanouts for what appears to be very different use cases.
But one of the benefits of working with Bright Data is we're...
Already working on adding that.

We don't have it today, but we're nimble enough to where we got it to our product team and are in the process of saying, okay, these are the keywords that were searched across Google, and then returned a search back into their RAG model, and then made their device say, hey, you should buy these tailor-made golf clubs.
So, yes, we can absolutely work with you on that, and then that would be returned in like a JSON CSV format.
You pick the format that you want it in, and then we return for it.
Okay, very cool.
And then kind of the last but not least is we have an IDE environment where you can develop and use our base code to scrape any website that's out there.

So, let's say, like an example of that is we're actually working with a company that wants to scrape every single assessor's website across every county, and obviously we don't have APIs built for every single assessor.
So we're able to then work with them on that IDE environment and say, hey, you can code based on this.
These counties, obviously, in Florida are very similar.
Here's the HTML format, build a code, how many it builds a scraper for you that you manage.
Now, the thing with that is any kind of a website changes, you're then responsible for changing that code to the site.
But you can also send us a list of websites and be like, go build scrapers for all these, and we can do that for you as well.
So it really comes down to what want to do in that.
Easiest way to get started is, one, create an account.
We don't require a credit card.
We don't require any type of like base.
Once that account is created, then Tom and I can, the background, add some credits for like a trial.
So you can actually like POC and get your hands dirty, hands-on keyboard experience with our platform.
And once we've got...

All that dialed and set up, I recommend doing a technical deep dive to say, okay, now that you know where to click on the API, like, where are you having issues or where are you having difficulties testing whether this is a viable solution for you, especially as we look at things like query fanout and building a decision tree for how these LLMs are loading themselves with bias.
And we want to change that bias so that you can add value to your customer and go on from there.
Excellent.
Yeah, no, that sounds great.
I'm a big fan of, you know, just digging in.
Cool.

One of the things that I'll recommend as we kind of do a POC or we like get you getting a free trial in testing is testing out some of the other components like our SERP product, which is our search engines results page.
Because at the end of the day, you know, like all, if you're talking with ChatGPT and you're saying, and you're saying.
Like, what are the best golf clubs that are happening?
Like, what ChatGPT is doing in the background is opening up 100 Google pages, doing a search across those SERP results, adding in certain keywords like, what are the best golf clubs?
What are the cheapest golf clubs?
What are the most forgiving?

That's going out and doing all those searches, scraping all of those engine results, then putting it through a RAG model based on what it's already learned and saying, okay, here is our recommendations for the clubs you should buy.
And so there's ways that I want to put more control in your hands of like, okay, how can you identify what's being scraped across all of those searches?
And then what's training the OpenAI model to then spit out the results it has.
then you can, that query fan out, you can then kind of do like side test models of like, okay, when I Google these things, what's coming up?
Because the query fan out shows you like what it's actually like Googling or searching for or putting.
Check out onto the open web to find that information.
I did a lot of rambling there.
I'm going to shut up now and see if any of that made sense and if any of that was helpful.
No, it totally makes sense to me.
Yeah, I actually did a little bit of this as a side project when I was on Spatical before I joined GrowthX just for fun.
I'm doing a little bit of research and scraping myself.
And when I was a dev, I used to do a little bit of that for competitive, yeah, analysis, you know, like Amazon asked you guys to do it.
My boss asked me to do a lot of that as a developer a long time ago.
But anyway, so, yeah, it all makes sense to me.
And it sounds like the licensing is really flexible.
Like you said that you don't, you know, have licensing for the data that's scraped.
That's right.
Thank you.
Thank
Um, yeah, and a key thing here is we're, we're nimble enough to work backwards from your economics model.
So let's say we like understand, like, there's obviously going to be certain customers that are probably more profitable in this space than others, right?
Cause it's like, you've got a customer that makes one request and like, we're happy.
You made our engine run perfectly for, for, uh, ChachiBT or Claude or whatever it be.
And then there's other customers who are like, we need a thousand different renditions of this.
And that makes it so that you have to do more searches and do more validation.
Um, when it comes to pricing, we are, our, our rate card is $1.50, but I have a lot of flexibility, $1.50 per 1000 scrapes.
And that's successful scrapes that doesn't, um, but we, most of my conversations with my customers are understanding, especially cause most of these.
Scraping tools are being added into their application stack, right, where then it's directly impacting their profitability of the customer using their application stack.
We can work with you very closely.
I'm like, okay, we know that you're going to be hitting these volumes and we know that you're charging this.
We can help you reduce that cost dramatically in order to make it more profitable for you, while at the same time more lucrative for us to ensure that there's a mutual benefit.
That you're not eating all the costs and having your costs go through the roof by, you know, un-tethered searches or scrapes.
Very good.
Yeah.
Yeah, this stuff can be very expensive.
Yeah, for sure.
Great.
Yeah, so do you have any use cases that you can share, or case studies you could share of other customers, you know, case studies that are publicly available, that are using your, your...

Scraper for similar use cases for, you know, understanding those, like, I know that you're kind of in flight with the AI visibility metrics and all of that, but has anybody kind of already worked with you guys on that and that you can share?

Yeah, from a public reference, I'll have to dig into that from just, like, anecdotal or what I can share to say based on what I'm, I'm, this, these types of conversations are taking up about, about 70% of my calendar right now.

So this is, at the end of the day, we're exploding right now because I can't say their names, but the largest AI models in the world right now are using us to be their public data source, both for how MCP is being used as, as well as training information.

So what I'll do in the background, or as a follow-up, is go through our public references, and then if necessary, I'm even happy to set up a private one-on-one conversation with you and one of our customers to establish a baseline for your specific use case.
Yeah, yeah.
Any information would be great, just because it's all a new area.
We're all kind of learning, you know, as we go along.
So, yeah, just curious about how other people are tackling, you know, really trying to understand brand visibility in these engines.
Perfect.

So what I'd recommend as a next step is in the background, Tom and I will look down public references, and then I'll email those to you, and then two, we'll have you in the background build an account.
Once you get the public references from us, just respond with your new account number, and we'll do some things in the background to make you, you know, that you're...
Yeah, thank you so much for the opportunity, we'll be in touch shortly, and my cell phone is always in my email signature, so feel free to text your call anytime.
All right, well thanks guys, take care.
thank you.
Bye.
Thanks Stevie.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Evaluate Bright Data as a data vendor for AI visibility metrics.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Core Value Prop:** Compliant data scraping (2.5 PB/day) via a contracted proxy network, proven by winning legal challenges from Elon Musk and Facebook.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [**Flexible Licensing:** GrowthX owns all scraped data in perpetuity, enabling redistribution to end customers without additional fees.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)
  - [**Key Feature Gap:** "Query fanout" (tracking AI's internal search queries) is in development but not yet available.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0)
  - [**Action Plan:** GrowthX to create a free account for a trial; Bright Data will provide public case studies and add trial credits upon receipt of the account number.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=1179.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX's Data Needs

  - [GrowthX is evaluating data vendors for a new initiative on AI visibility metrics.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)
  - [**Required Data:**](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=220.0)
      - [AI mentions & citations](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0)
      - [Keyword & search volume](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=220.0)
  - [**Key Evaluation Criteria:**](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=220.0)
      - [AI platform coverage (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude)](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
      - [Data collection methodology (e.g., panel vs. probing)](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=232.0)
      - [Pricing & licensing for embedding and redistributing data to end customers](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=259.0)

### Bright Data's Solution

  - [**Scale & Compliance:**](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
      - [Scrapes \~2.5 petabytes of data daily.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0)
      - [Strict compliance (no login screens, transparent ToS) → won legal challenges from Elon Musk and Facebook.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=314.0)
  - [**Technical Foundation:**](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=383.0)
      - [Built on the world's largest proxy network, where every device is contracted for reliability.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=383.0)
      - [All tools are API-based, charged per successful record (not 404s).](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=458.0)
  - [**Tooling Options:**](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
      - [**Pre-built APIs:** For popular sites (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude, Amazon).](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
      - [**Browser APIs & Unlockers:** To bypass blockers on sites without dedicated APIs.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=592.0)
      - [**IDE Environment:** For building custom scrapers (client-managed maintenance).](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=697.0)
      - [**Managed Service:** Bright Data can build and maintain custom scrapers for a list of sites.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)

### Licensing & Pricing

  - [**Licensing:** GrowthX owns all scraped data in perpetuity, with full rights to redistribute it to end customers without additional fees.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)
  - [**Pricing:**](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=997.0)
      - [Standard rate card: $1.50 per 1,000 successful scrapes.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=997.0)
      - [Flexible pricing for embedded use cases to ensure client profitability.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)

### Feature Gap: Query Fanout

  - [**Need:** Track the internal search queries an AI model makes to generate its answer.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=817.0)
  - [**Status:** Not currently available.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0)
  - [**Rationale:** High customer interest (70% of Mike's calls) → product team is actively developing it.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=1099.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Bright Data:**](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=1179.0)
      - [Provide public case studies on AI-related scraping.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=1065.0)
      - [Add trial credits to GrowthX's account upon receiving the account number.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=1179.0)
  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=1179.0)
      - [Create a free Bright Data account.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=756.0)
      - [Share the account number with Bright Data to receive trial credits.](https://fathom.video/share/jTDbXkBszoDGK9YqEXLsKrGs824zfZGc?tab=summary&timestamp=1179.0)

action_items.description: Email Stevie public AI/SERP case studies; if none, propose private 1:1 ref
Create Bright Data account; email account # to Michael/Tam for trial credits
